more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Betting Platforms Dominate Zimbabwe’s Most Popular Google Searches
more.description: Some days ago, I saw on X that said MWOS, a betting company, has +101 branches in Zimbabwe. Quite significant for our small country. So I checked Google Trends to see how betting is doing online. The numbers there are something else. Betting-related searches (for words like MWOS, AfricaBet, Premier Bet, BolaBet, Spin City, and [...]

more.title: Remove ‘Irrational’ Fuel Taxes Now Or Risk Unrest: ZCTU Warns Govt As Transport Fares Skyrocket
more.description: Remove Fuel Taxes Now: ZCTU Issues Stark Warning To Government Zimbabwe’s largest labour body has warned authorities to urgently remove what it calls “irrational” fuel taxes or risk unrest, as transport fares surge sharply following recent fuel price increases that have left commuters struggling to cope. more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe finds itself at a critical juncture, grappling with a contentious proposal to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s tenure until 2030. more.title: Mutapa’s New Prize: The Secret Plan Behind ZISCOSTEEL’s Sudden Move to the Sovereign Wealth Fund
more.description: The legendary Zimbabwe Iron and Steel Company (ZISCOSTEEL), once the undisputed pride of African industry, has been quietly moved under the umbrella of the Mutapa Investment Fund. But what does this sudden transfer mean for the thousands of former workers left destitute, and for the nation’s industrial future?
